verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is synthesized as a latent precursor (pro-TGFB1) containing a signal peptide, which is secreted into the extracellular space. The active TGF-β ligand is released via proteolytic cleavage, but the latent complex (LAP-TGFB1) remains associated with extracellular matrix and cell surfaces through covalent and non-covalent interactions with TM partners and matrix components. Additionally, TGFB1 is shed from cell surfaces and can be displayed on outer leaflets of activated immune cells and dying cells (immunogenic cell death context). The protein transitions between secreted-soluble and cell-surface-associated pools depending on cellular state and tissue context.
